17	package hack
18	
19	import (
20		"fmt"
21		"path/filepath"
22		"time"
23	
24		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
25	)
26	
27	// DefaultBoilerplatePath is the default path to the boilerplate file
28	var DefaultBoilerplatePath = filepath.Join("hack", "boilerplate.go.txt")
29	
30	var _ machinery.Template = &Boilerplate{}
31	
32	// Boilerplate scaffolds a file that defines the common header for the rest of the files
33	type Boilerplate struct {
34		machinery.TemplateMixin
35		machinery.BoilerplateMixin
36	
37		// License is the License type to write
38		License string
39	
40		// Licenses maps License types to their actual string
41		Licenses map[string]string
42	
43		// Owner is the copyright owner - e.g. "The Kubernetes Authors"
44		Owner string
45	
46		// Year is the copyright year
47		Year string
48	}
49	
50	// Validate implements file.RequiresValidation
51	func (f *Boilerplate) Validate() error {
52		if f.License != "" {
53			if _, foundKnown := knownLicenses[f.License]; !foundKnown {
54				if _, found := f.Licenses[f.License]; !found {
55					return fmt.Errorf("unknown specified license %s", f.License)
56				}
57			}
58		}
59		return nil
60	}
61	
62	// SetTemplateDefaults implements machinery.Template
63	func (f *Boilerplate) SetTemplateDefaults() error {
64		if f.Path == "" {
65			f.Path = DefaultBoilerplatePath
66		}
67	
68		if f.License == "" {
69			f.License = "apache2"
70		}
71	
72		if f.Licenses == nil {
73			f.Licenses = make(map[string]string, len(knownLicenses))
74		}
75	
76		for key, value := range knownLicenses {
77			if _, hasLicense := f.Licenses[key]; !hasLicense {
78				f.Licenses[key] = value
79			}
80		}
81	
82		if f.Year == "" {
83			f.Year = fmt.Sprintf("%v", time.Now().Year())
84		}
85	
86		// Boilerplate given
87		if len(f.Boilerplate) > 0 {
88			f.TemplateBody = f.Boilerplate
89			return nil
90		}
91	
92		f.TemplateBody = boilerplateTemplate
93	
94		return nil
95	}
96	
97	const boilerplateTemplate = `/*
98	{{ if .Owner -}}
99	Copyright {{ .Year }} {{ .Owner }}.
100	{{- else -}}
101	Copyright {{ .Year }}.
102	{{- end }}
103	{{ index .Licenses .License }}*/`
104	
105	var knownLicenses = map[string]string{

108	}

content.text: Now let me understand the full picture. I need to look at how the boilerplate is used, the machinery package for boilerplate injection, and the Makefile template

content.content:
1	/*
2	Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package templates
18	
19	import (
20		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
21	)
22	
23	var _ machinery.Template = &Makefile{}
24	
25	// Makefile scaffolds a file that defines project management CLI commands
26	type Makefile struct {
27		machinery.TemplateMixin
28		machinery.ProjectNameMixin
29	
30		// Image is controller manager image name
31		Image string
32		// BoilerplatePath is the path to the boilerplate file
33		BoilerplatePath string
34		// Controller tools version to use in the project
35		ControllerToolsVersion string
36		// Kustomize version to use in the project
37		KustomizeVersion string
38		// golangci-lint version to use in the project
39		GolangciLintVersion string
40		// ControllerRuntimeVersion version to be used to download the envtest setup script
41		ControllerRuntimeVersion string
42		// EnvtestVersion store the name of the verions to be used to install setup-envtest
43		EnvtestVersion string
44	}
45	
46	// SetTemplateDefaults implements machinery.Template
47	func (f *Makefile) SetTemplateDefaults() error {
48		if f.Path == "" {
49			f.Path = "Makefile"
50		}
51	
52		f.TemplateBody = makefileTemplate
53	
54		f.IfExistsAction = machinery.Error
55	
56		if f.Image == "" {
57			f.Image = "controller:latest"
58		}
59	
60		// TODO: Current workaround for setup-envtest compatibility
61		// Due to past instances where controller-runtime maintainers released
62		// versions without corresponding branches, directly relying on branches
63		// poses a risk of breaking the Kubebuilder chain. Such practices may
64		// change over time, potentially leading to compatibility issues. This
65		// approach, although not ideal, remains the best solution for ensuring
66		// compatibility with controller-runtime releases as of now. For more
67		// details on the quest for a more robust solution, refer to the issue
68		// raised in the controller-runtime repository: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/2744
69		if f.EnvtestVersion == "" {
70			f.EnvtestVersion = "latest"
71		}
72		return nil
73	}
74	
75	//nolint:lll
76	const makefileTemplate = `# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
77	IMG ?= {{ .Image }}
78	
79	# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
80	ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
81	GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
82	else
83	GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
84	endif
85	
86	# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
87	# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
88	# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
89	# tools. (i.e. podman)
90	CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker
91	
92	# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
93	# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
94	SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
95	.SHELLFLAGS = -ec
96	
97	.PHONY: all
98	all: build
99	
100	##@ General
101	
102	# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
103	# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '##@' and the
104	# target descriptions by '##'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
105	# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
106	# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
107	# if there's a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
108	# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
109	# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
110	# More info on the awk command:
111	# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php
112	
113	.PHONY: help
114	help: ## Display this help.
115		@awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)
116	
117	##@ Development
118	
119	.PHONY: manifests
120	manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
121		"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases
122	
123	.PHONY: generate
124	generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
125		{{ if .BoilerplatePath -}}
126		"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile={{printf "%q" .BoilerplatePath}} paths="./..."
127		{{- else -}}
128		"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object paths="./..."
129		{{- end }}
130	
131	.PHONY: fmt
132	fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
133		go fmt ./...
134	
135	.PHONY: vet
136	vet: ## Run go vet against code.
137		go vet ./...
138	
139	.PHONY: test
140	test: manifests generate fmt vet setup-envtest ## Run tests.
141		KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(shell "$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path)" go test $$(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out
142	
143	# TODO(user): To use a different vendor for e2e tests, modify the setup under 'tests/e2e'.
144	# The default setup assumes Kind is pre-installed and builds/loads the Manager Docker image locally.
145	# CertManager is installed by default; skip with:
146	# - CERT_MANAGER_INSTALL_SKIP=true
147	KIND_CLUSTER ?= {{ .ProjectName }}-test-e2e
148	
149	.PHONY: setup-test-e2e
150	setup-test-e2e: ## Set up a Kind cluster for e2e tests if it does not exist
151		@command -v $(KIND) >/dev/null 2>&1 || { \
152			echo "Kind is not installed. Please install Kind manually."; \
153			exit 1; \
154		}
155		@case "$$($(KIND) get clusters)" in \
156			*"$(KIND_CLUSTER)"*) \
157				echo "Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)' already exists. Skipping creation." ;; \
158			*) \
159				echo "Creating Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)'..."; \
160				$(KIND) create cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER) ;; \
161		esac
162	
163	.PHONY: test-e2e
164	test-e2e: setup-test-e2e manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the e2e tests. Expected an isolated environment using Kind.
165		KIND=$(KIND) KIND_CLUSTER=$(KIND_CLUSTER) go test -tags=e2e ./test/e2e/ -v -ginkgo.v
166		$(MAKE) cleanup-test-e2e
167	
168	.PHONY: cleanup-test-e2e
169	cleanup-test-e2e: ## Tear down the Kind cluster used for e2e tests
170		@$(KIND) delete cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER)
171	
172	.PHONY: lint
173	lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter
174		"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run
175	
176	.PHONY: lint-fix
177	lint-fix: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter and perform fixes
178		"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run --fix
179	
180	.PHONY: lint-config
181	lint-config: golangci-lint ## Verify golangci-lint linter configuration
182		"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" config verify
183	
184	##@ Build
185	
186	.PHONY: build
187	build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
188		go build -o bin/manager cmd/main.go
189	
190	.PHONY: run
191	run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
192		go run ./cmd/main.go
193	
194	# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
195	# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
196	# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
197	.PHONY: docker-build
198	docker-build: ## Build docker image with the manager.
199		$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -t ${IMG} .
200	
201	.PHONY: docker-push
202	docker-push: ## Push docker image with the manager.
203		$(CONTAINER_TOOL) push ${IMG}
204	
205	# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for the manager image be built to provide support to multiple
206	# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
207	# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
208	# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
209	# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
210	# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
211	PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
212	.PHONY: docker-buildx
213	docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
214		# copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
215		sed -e '1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t' -e ' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
216		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
217		$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
218		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
219		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
220		rm Dockerfile.cross
221	
222	.PHONY: build-installer
223	build-installer: manifests generate kustomize ## Generate a consolidated YAML with CRDs and deployment.
224		mkdir -p dist
225		cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
226		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml
227	
228	##@ Deployment
229	
230	ifndef ignore-not-found
231	  ignore-not-found = false
232	endif
233	
234	.PHONY: install
235	install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
236		@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
237		if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi
238	
239	.PHONY: uninstall
240	uninstall: manifests kustomize ## Uninstall CRDs from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
241		@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
242		if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi
243	
244	.PHONY: deploy
245	deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
246		cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
247		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -
248	
249	.PHONY: undeploy
250	undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
251		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -
252	
253	##@ Dependencies
254	
255	## Location to install dependencies to
256	LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
257	$(LOCALBIN):
258		mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"
259	
260	## Tool Binaries
261	KUBECTL ?= kubectl
262	KIND ?= kind
263	KUSTOMIZE ?= $(LOCALBIN)/kustomize
264	CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
265	ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest
266	GOLANGCI_LINT = $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint
267	
268	## Tool Versions
269	KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= {{ .KustomizeVersion }}
270	CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= {{ .ControllerToolsVersion }}
271	
272	#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
273	ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'; \
274	  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
275	  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/')
276	
277	#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
278	ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'; \
279	  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
280	  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/')
281	
282	GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= {{ .GolangciLintVersion }}
283	.PHONY: kustomize
284	kustomize: $(KUSTOMIZE) ## Download kustomize locally if necessary.
285	$(KUSTOMIZE): $(LOCALBIN)
286		$(call go-install-tool,$(KUSTOMIZE),sigs.k8s.io/kustomize/kustomize/v5,$(KUSTOMIZE_VERSION))
287	
288	.PHONY: controller-gen
289	controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
290	$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
291		$(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))
292	
293	.PHONY: setup-envtest
294	setup-envtest: envtest ## Download the binaries required for ENVTEST in the local bin directory.
295		@echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
296		@"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
297			echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \
298			exit 1; \
299		}
300	
301	.PHONY: envtest
302	envtest: $(ENVTEST) ## Download setup-envtest locally if necessary.
303	$(ENVTEST): $(LOCALBIN)
304		$(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))
305	
306	.PHONY: golangci-lint
307	golangci-lint: $(GOLANGCI_LINT) ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
308	$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
309		$(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
310		@test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
311			echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
312			$(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
313			mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
314		} || true
315	
316	# go-install-tool will 'go install' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn't exist
317	# $1 - target path with name of binary
318	# $2 - package url which can be installed
319	# $3 - specific version of package
320	define go-install-tool
321	@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
322	set -e; \
323	package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
324	echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
325	rm -f "$(1)" ;\
326	GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
327	mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
328	} ;\
329	ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
330	endef
331	
332	define gomodver
333	$(shell go list -m -f '{{"{{"}}if .Replace{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Replace.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}else{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}end{{"}}"}}' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
334	endef
335	`
336

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

/*
Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
*/

package templates

import (
	"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
)

var _ machinery.Template = &Makefile{}

// Makefile scaffolds a file that defines project management CLI commands
type Makefile struct {
	machinery.TemplateMixin
	machinery.ProjectNameMixin

	// Image is controller manager image name
	Image string
	// BoilerplatePath is the path to the boilerplate file
	BoilerplatePath string
	// Controller tools version to use in the project
	ControllerToolsVersion string
	// Kustomize version to use in the project
	KustomizeVersion string
	// golangci-lint version to use in the project
	GolangciLintVersion string
	// ControllerRuntimeVersion version to be used to download the envtest setup script
	ControllerRuntimeVersion string
	// EnvtestVersion store the name of the verions to be used to install setup-envtest
	EnvtestVersion string
}

// SetTemplateDefaults implements machinery.Template
func (f *Makefile) SetTemplateDefaults() error {
	if f.Path == "" {
		f.Path = "Makefile"
	}

	f.TemplateBody = makefileTemplate

	f.IfExistsAction = machinery.Error

	if f.Image == "" {
		f.Image = "controller:latest"
	}

	// TODO: Current workaround for setup-envtest compatibility
	// Due to past instances where controller-runtime maintainers released
	// versions without corresponding branches, directly relying on branches
	// poses a risk of breaking the Kubebuilder chain. Such practices may
	// change over time, potentially leading to compatibility issues. This
	// approach, although not ideal, remains the best solution for ensuring
	// compatibility with controller-runtime releases as of now. For more
	// details on the quest for a more robust solution, refer to the issue
	// raised in the controller-runtime repository: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/2744
	if f.EnvtestVersion == "" {
		f.EnvtestVersion = "latest"
	}
	return nil
}

//nolint:lll
const makefileTemplate = `# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
IMG ?= {{ .Image }}

# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
else
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
endif

# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
# tools. (i.e. podman)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS = -ec

.PHONY: all
all: build

##@ General

# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '##@' and the
# target descriptions by '##'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
# if there's a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
# More info on the awk command:
# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
	@awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

##@ Development

.PHONY: manifests
manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
	"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases

.PHONY: generate
generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
	{{ if .BoilerplatePath -}}
	"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile={{printf "%q" .BoilerplatePath}} paths="./..."
	{{- else -}}
	"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object paths="./..."
	{{- end }}

.PHONY: fmt
fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
	go fmt ./...

.PHONY: vet
vet: ## Run go vet against code.
	go vet ./...

.PHONY: test
test: manifests generate fmt vet setup-envtest ## Run tests.
	KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(shell "$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path)" go test $$(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out

# TODO(user): To use a different vendor for e2e tests, modify the setup under 'tests/e2e'.
# The default setup assumes Kind is pre-installed and builds/loads the Manager Docker image locally.
# CertManager is installed by default; skip with:
# - CERT_MANAGER_INSTALL_SKIP=true
KIND_CLUSTER ?= {{ .ProjectName }}-test-e2e

.PHONY: setup-test-e2e
setup-test-e2e: ## Set up a Kind cluster for e2e tests if it does not exist
	@command -v $(KIND) >/dev/null 2>&1 || { \
		echo "Kind is not installed. Please install Kind manually."; \
		exit 1; \
	}
	@case "$$($(KIND) get clusters)" in \
		*"$(KIND_CLUSTER)"*) \
			echo "Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)' already exists. Skipping creation." ;; \
		*) \
			echo "Creating Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)'..."; \
			$(KIND) create cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER) ;; \
	esac

.PHONY: test-e2e
test-e2e: setup-test-e2e manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the e2e tests. Expected an isolated environment using Kind.
	KIND=$(KIND) KIND_CLUSTER=$(KIND_CLUSTER) go test -tags=e2e ./test/e2e/ -v -ginkgo.v
	$(MAKE) cleanup-test-e2e

.PHONY: cleanup-test-e2e
cleanup-test-e2e: ## Tear down the Kind cluster used for e2e tests
	@$(KIND) delete cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER)

.PHONY: lint
lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run

.PHONY: lint-fix
lint-fix: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter and perform fixes
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run --fix

.PHONY: lint-config
lint-config: golangci-lint ## Verify golangci-lint linter configuration
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" config verify

##@ Build

.PHONY: build
build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
	go build -o bin/manager cmd/main.go

.PHONY: run
run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
	go run ./cmd/main.go

# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
.PHONY: docker-build
docker-build: ## Build docker image with the manager.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -t ${IMG} .

.PHONY: docker-push
docker-push: ## Push docker image with the manager.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) push ${IMG}

# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for the manager image be built to provide support to multiple
# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
.PHONY: docker-buildx
docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
	# copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
	sed -e '1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t' -e ' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
	rm Dockerfile.cross

.PHONY: build-installer
build-installer: manifests generate kustomize ## Generate a consolidated YAML with CRDs and deployment.
	mkdir -p dist
	cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml

##@ Deployment

ifndef ignore-not-found
  ignore-not-found = false
endif

.PHONY: install
install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
	@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
	if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: uninstall
uninstall: manifests kustomize ## Uninstall CRDs from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
	@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
	if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: deploy
deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
	cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -

.PHONY: undeploy
undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -

##@ Dependencies

## Location to install dependencies to
LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
$(LOCALBIN):
	mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"

## Tool Binaries
KUBECTL ?= kubectl
KIND ?= kind
KUSTOMIZE ?= $(LOCALBIN)/kustomize
CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest
GOLANGCI_LINT = $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint

## Tool Versions
KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= {{ .KustomizeVersion }}
CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= {{ .ControllerToolsVersion }}

#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/')

#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/')

GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= {{ .GolangciLintVersion }}
.PHONY: kustomize
kustomize: $(KUSTOMIZE) ## Download kustomize locally if necessary.
$(KUSTOMIZE): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(KUSTOMIZE),sigs.k8s.io/kustomize/kustomize/v5,$(KUSTOMIZE_VERSION))

.PHONY: controller-gen
controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))

.PHONY: setup-envtest
setup-envtest: envtest ## Download the binaries required for ENVTEST in the local bin directory.
	@echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
	@"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
		echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \
		exit 1; \
	}

.PHONY: envtest
envtest: $(ENVTEST) ## Download setup-envtest locally if necessary.
$(ENVTEST): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))

.PHONY: golangci-lint
golangci-lint: $(GOLANGCI_LINT) ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
	@test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
		echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
		$(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
		mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
	} || true

# go-install-tool will 'go install' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn't exist
# $1 - target path with name of binary
# $2 - package url which can be installed
# $3 - specific version of package
define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
rm -f "$(1)" ;\
GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
} ;\
ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
endef

define gomodver
$(shell go list -m -f '{{"{{"}}if .Replace{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Replace.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}else{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}end{{"}}"}}' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
endef
`

114	
115	# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
116	# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.

118	.PHONY: docker-build

127	# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
128	# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
129	# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
130	# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
131	# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
132	PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
133	.PHONY: docker-buildx
134	docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
135		# copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
136		sed -e '1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t' -e ' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
137		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name project-v4-builder
138		$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use project-v4-builder
139		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
140		- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm project-v4-builder
141		rm Dockerfile.cross

143	.PHONY: build-installer

145		mkdir -p dist
146		cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
147		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml

151	ifndef ignore-not-found
152	  ignore-not-found = false
153	endif

156	install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
157		@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
158		if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi
159

162		@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
163		if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi
164	
165	.PHONY: deploy
166	deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
167		cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
168		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -
169

171	undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
172		"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -
173	
174	##@ Dependencies
175	
176	## Location to install dependencies to
177	LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
178	$(LOCALBIN):
179		mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"

181	## Tool Binaries
182	KUBECTL ?= kubectl
183	KIND ?= kind
184	KUSTOMIZE ?= $(LOCALBIN)/kustomize
185	CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
186	ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest
187	GOLANGCI_LINT = $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint
188

190	KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= v5.8.1
191	CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.20.1
192	
193	#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
194	ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'; \
195	  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
196	  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/')
197	
198	#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
199	ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'; \
200	  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
201	  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/')
202	
203	GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= v2.8.0
204	.PHONY: kustomize
205	kustomize: $(KUSTOMIZE) ## Download kustomize locally if necessary.
206	$(KUSTOMIZE): $(LOCALBIN)
207		$(call go-install-tool,$(KUSTOMIZE),sigs.k8s.io/kustomize/kustomize/v5,$(KUSTOMIZE_VERSION))
208

210	controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
211	$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
212		$(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))
213

215	setup-envtest: envtest ## Download the binaries required for ENVTEST in the local bin directory.
216		@echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
217		@"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
218			echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \
219			exit 1; \
220		}
221	
222	.PHONY: envtest
223	envtest: $(ENVTEST) ## Download setup-envtest locally if necessary.
224	$(ENVTEST): $(LOCALBIN)
225		$(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))
226	
227	.PHONY: golangci-lint
228	golangci-lint: $(GOLANGCI_LINT) ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
229	$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
230		$(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
231		@test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
232			echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
233			$(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
234			mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
235		} || true
236	
237	# go-install-tool will 'go install' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn't exist
238	# $1 - target path with name of binary
239	# $2 - package url which can be installed
240	# $3 - specific version of package
241	define go-install-tool
242	@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
243	set -e; \
244	package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
245	echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
246	rm -f "$(1)" ;\
247	GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
248	mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
249	} ;\
250	ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
251	endef
252	
253	define gomodver
254	$(shell go list -m -f '{{if .Replace}}{{.Replace.Version}}{{else}}{{.Version}}{{end}}' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
255	endef

file.content:
# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
IMG ?= controller:latest

# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
else
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
endif

# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
# tools. (i.e. podman)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS = -ec

.PHONY: all
all: build

##@ General

# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '##@' and the
# target descriptions by '##'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
# if there's a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
# More info on the awk command:
# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
	@awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

##@ Development

.PHONY: manifests
manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
	"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases

.PHONY: generate
generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
	"$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile="hack/boilerplate.go.txt" paths="./..."

.PHONY: fmt
fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
	go fmt ./...

.PHONY: vet
vet: ## Run go vet against code.
	go vet ./...

.PHONY: test
test: manifests generate fmt vet setup-envtest ## Run tests.
	KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(shell "$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path)" go test $$(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out

# TODO(user): To use a different vendor for e2e tests, modify the setup under 'tests/e2e'.
# The default setup assumes Kind is pre-installed and builds/loads the Manager Docker image locally.
# CertManager is installed by default; skip with:
# - CERT_MANAGER_INSTALL_SKIP=true
KIND_CLUSTER ?= project-v4-test-e2e

.PHONY: setup-test-e2e
setup-test-e2e: ## Set up a Kind cluster for e2e tests if it does not exist
	@command -v $(KIND) >/dev/null 2>&1 || { \
		echo "Kind is not installed. Please install Kind manually."; \
		exit 1; \
	}
	@case "$$($(KIND) get clusters)" in \
		*"$(KIND_CLUSTER)"*) \
			echo "Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)' already exists. Skipping creation." ;; \
		*) \
			echo "Creating Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)'..."; \
			$(KIND) create cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER) ;; \
	esac

.PHONY: test-e2e
test-e2e: setup-test-e2e manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the e2e tests. Expected an isolated environment using Kind.
	KIND=$(KIND) KIND_CLUSTER=$(KIND_CLUSTER) go test -tags=e2e ./test/e2e/ -v -ginkgo.v
	$(MAKE) cleanup-test-e2e

.PHONY: cleanup-test-e2e
cleanup-test-e2e: ## Tear down the Kind cluster used for e2e tests
	@$(KIND) delete cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER)

.PHONY: lint
lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run

.PHONY: lint-fix
lint-fix: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter and perform fixes
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run --fix

.PHONY: lint-config
lint-config: golangci-lint ## Verify golangci-lint linter configuration
	"$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" config verify

##@ Build

.PHONY: build
build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
	go build -o bin/manager cmd/main.go

.PHONY: run
run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
	go run ./cmd/main.go

# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
.PHONY: docker-build
docker-build: ## Build docker image with the manager.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -t ${IMG} .

.PHONY: docker-push
docker-push: ## Push docker image with the manager.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) push ${IMG}

# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for the manager image be built to provide support to multiple
# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
.PHONY: docker-buildx
docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
	# copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
	sed -e '1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t' -e ' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name project-v4-builder
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use project-v4-builder
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
	- $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm project-v4-builder
	rm Dockerfile.cross

.PHONY: build-installer
build-installer: manifests generate kustomize ## Generate a consolidated YAML with CRDs and deployment.
	mkdir -p dist
	cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml

##@ Deployment

ifndef ignore-not-found
  ignore-not-found = false
endif

.PHONY: install
install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
	@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
	if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: uninstall
uninstall: manifests kustomize ## Uninstall CRDs from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
	@out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
	if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: deploy
deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
	cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -

.PHONY: undeploy
undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
	"$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -

##@ Dependencies

## Location to install dependencies to
LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
$(LOCALBIN):
	mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"

## Tool Binaries
KUBECTL ?= kubectl
KIND ?= kind
KUSTOMIZE ?= $(LOCALBIN)/kustomize
CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest
GOLANGCI_LINT = $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint

## Tool Versions
KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= v5.8.1
CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.20.1

#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/')

#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/')

GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= v2.8.0
.PHONY: kustomize
kustomize: $(KUSTOMIZE) ## Download kustomize locally if necessary.
$(KUSTOMIZE): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(KUSTOMIZE),sigs.k8s.io/kustomize/kustomize/v5,$(KUSTOMIZE_VERSION))

.PHONY: controller-gen
controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))

.PHONY: setup-envtest
setup-envtest: envtest ## Download the binaries required for ENVTEST in the local bin directory.
	@echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
	@"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
		echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \
		exit 1; \
	}

.PHONY: envtest
envtest: $(ENVTEST) ## Download setup-envtest locally if necessary.
$(ENVTEST): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))

.PHONY: golangci-lint
golangci-lint: $(GOLANGCI_LINT) ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
	@test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
		echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
		$(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
		mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
	} || true

# go-install-tool will 'go install' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn't exist
# $1 - target path with name of binary
# $2 - package url which can be installed
# $3 - specific version of package
define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
rm -f "$(1)" ;\
GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
} ;\
ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
endef

define gomodver
$(shell go list -m -f '{{if .Replace}}{{.Replace.Version}}{{else}}{{.Version}}{{end}}' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
endef

31		kustomizecommonv2 "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/common/kustomize/v2"
32		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates"
33		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/cmd"
34		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/github"
35		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/hack"

40	const (
41		// GolangciLintVersion is the golangci-lint version to be used in the project

43		// ControllerRuntimeVersion is the kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime version to be used in the project

45		// ControllerToolsVersion is the kubernetes-sigs/controller-tools version to be used in the project

51	var _ plugins.Scaffolder = &initScaffolder{}
52

54	
55	type initScaffolder struct {
56		config          config.Config
57		boilerplatePath string
58		license         string
59		owner           string
60		commandName     string
61	
62		// fs is the filesystem that will be used by the scaffolder
63		fs machinery.Filesystem
64	}
65	
66	// NewInitScaffolder returns a new Scaffolder for project initialization operations
67	func NewInitScaffolder(cfg config.Config, license, owner, commandName string) plugins.Scaffolder {
68		return &initScaffolder{
69			config:          cfg,
70			boilerplatePath: hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath,
71			license:         license,
72			owner:           owner,
73			commandName:     commandName,
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// InjectFS implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
78	func (s *initScaffolder) InjectFS(fs machinery.Filesystem) {
79		s.fs = fs
80	}
81	
82	// getControllerRuntimeReleaseBranch converts the ControllerRuntime semantic versioning string to a
83	// release branch string. Example input: "v0.17.0" -> Output: "release-0.17"
84	func getControllerRuntimeReleaseBranch() string {
85		v := strings.TrimPrefix(ControllerRuntimeVersion, "v")
86		tmp := strings.Split(v, ".")
87	
88		if len(tmp) < 2 {
89			fmt.Println("Invalid version format. Expected at least major and minor version numbers.")
90			return ""
91		}
92		releaseBranch := fmt.Sprintf("release-%s.%s", tmp[0], tmp[1])
93		return releaseBranch
94	}
95	
96	// Scaffold implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
97	func (s *initScaffolder) Scaffold() error {
98		log.Info("Writing scaffold for you to edit...")
99	
100		// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the boilerplate file to disk
101		// The boilerplate file needs to be scaffolded as a separate step as it is going to
102		// be used by the rest of the files, even those scaffolded in this command call.
103		scaffold := machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
104			machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
105		)
106	
107		if s.license != "none" {
108			bpFile := &hack.Boilerplate{
109				License: s.license,
110				Owner:   s.owner,
111			}
112			bpFile.Path = s.boilerplatePath
113			if err := scaffold.Execute(bpFile); err != nil {
114				return fmt.Errorf("failed to execute boilerplate: %w", err)
115			}
116	
117			boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, s.boilerplatePath)
118			if err != nil {
119				if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
120					log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
121						"This file is used to generate the license header in the project.\n"+
122						"Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
123						"add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
124						"file_path", s.boilerplatePath,
125						"error", err)
126					boilerplate = []byte("")
127				} else {
128					return fmt.Errorf("failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
129				}
130			}
131			// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
132			scaffold = machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
133				machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
134				machinery.WithBoilerplate(string(boilerplate)),
135			)
136		} else {
137			s.boilerplatePath = ""
138			// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold without boilerplate
139			scaffold = machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
140				machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
141			)
142		}
143	
144		// If the KustomizeV2 was used to do the scaffold then
145		// we need to ensure that we use its supported Kustomize Version
146		// in order to support it
147		kustomizev2 := kustomizecommonv2.Plugin{}
148		gov4 := "go.kubebuilder.io/v4"
149		pluginKeyForKustomizeV2 := plugin.KeyFor(kustomizev2)
150	
151		for _, pluginKey := range s.config.GetPluginChain() {
152			if pluginKey == pluginKeyForKustomizeV2 || pluginKey == gov4 {
153				kustomizeVersion = kustomizecommonv2.KustomizeVersion
154				break
155			}
156		}
157	
158		err := scaffold.Execute(
159			&cmd.Main{
160				ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion,
161			},
162			&templates.GoMod{
163				ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion,
164			},
165			&templates.GitIgnore{},
166			&templates.Makefile{
167				Image:                    imageName,
168				BoilerplatePath:          s.boilerplatePath,
169				ControllerToolsVersion:   ControllerToolsVersion,
170				KustomizeVersion:         kustomizeVersion,
171				GolangciLintVersion:      GolangciLintVersion,
172				ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion,
173				EnvtestVersion:           getControllerRuntimeReleaseBranch(),
174			},
175			&templates.Dockerfile{},
176			&templates.DockerIgnore{},
177			&templates.Readme{CommandName: s.commandName},
178			&templates.Agents{CommandName: s.commandName},
179			&templates.Golangci{},
180			&templates.CustomGcl{
181				GolangciLintVersion: GolangciLintVersion,
182			},
183			&e2e.Test{},
184			&e2e.WebhookTestUpdater{WireWebhook: false},
185			&e2e.SuiteTest{},
186			&github.E2eTestCi{},
187			&github.TestCi{},
188			&github.LintCi{
189				GolangciLintVersion: GolangciLintVersion,
190			},
191			&utils.Utils{},
192			&templates.DevContainer{},
193			&templates.DevContainerPostInstallScript{},
194		)
195		if err != nil {
196			return fmt.Errorf("failed to execute init scaffold: %w", err)
197		}
198	
199		return nil
200	}
201

16	
17	package scaffolds
18	
19	import (
20		"errors"
21		"fmt"
22		log "log/slog"
23	
24		"github.com/spf13/afero"
25	
26		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/config"
27		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
28		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/model/resource"
29		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins"
30		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/api"
31		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/cmd"
32		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/controllers"
33		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/hack"
34	)
35	
36	var _ plugins.Scaffolder = &apiScaffolder{}
37	
38	// apiScaffolder contains configuration for generating scaffolding for Go type
39	// representing the API and controller that implements the behavior for the API.
40	type apiScaffolder struct {
41		config   config.Config
42		resource resource.Resource
43	
44		// fs is the filesystem that will be used by the scaffolder
45		fs machinery.Filesystem
46	
47		// force indicates whether to scaffold controller files even if it exists or not
48		force bool
49	}
50	
51	// NewAPIScaffolder returns a new Scaffolder for API/controller creation operations
52	func NewAPIScaffolder(cfg config.Config, res resource.Resource, force bool) plugins.Scaffolder {
53		return &apiScaffolder{
54			config:   cfg,
55			resource: res,
56			force:    force,
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// InjectFS implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
61	func (s *apiScaffolder) InjectFS(fs machinery.Filesystem) {
62		s.fs = fs
63	}
64	
65	// Scaffold implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
66	func (s *apiScaffolder) Scaffold() error {
67		log.Info("Writing scaffold for you to edit...")
68	
69		// Load the boilerplate
70		boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath)
71		if err != nil {
72			if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
73				log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
74					"This file is used to generate the license header in the project.\n"+
75					"Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
76					"add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
77					"file_path", hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath, "error", err)
78				boilerplate = []byte("")
79			} else {
80				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding API/controller: failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
81			}
82		}
83	
84		// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
85		scaffold := machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
86			machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
87			machinery.WithBoilerplate(string(boilerplate)),
88			machinery.WithResource(&s.resource),
89		)
90	
91		// Keep track of these values before the update
92		doAPI := s.resource.HasAPI()
93		doController := s.resource.HasController()
94	
95		if err := s.config.UpdateResource(s.resource); err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("error updating resource: %w", err)
97		}
98	
99		if doAPI {
100			if err := scaffold.Execute(
101				&api.Types{Force: s.force},
102				&api.Group{},
103			); err != nil {
104				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding APIs: %w", err)
105			}
106		}
107	
108		if doController {
109			if err := scaffold.Execute(
110				&controllers.SuiteTest{Force: s.force},
111				&controllers.Controller{ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion, Force: s.force},
112				&controllers.ControllerTest{Force: s.force, DoAPI: doAPI},
113			); err != nil {
114				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding controller: %w", err)
115			}
116		}
117	
118		if err := scaffold.Execute(
119			&cmd.MainUpdater{WireResource: doAPI, WireController: doController},
120		); err != nil {
121			return fmt.Errorf("error updating cmd/main.go: %w", err)
122		}
123	
124		return nil
125	}
126

17	package scaffolds
18	
19	import (
20		"errors"

22		log "log/slog"
23		"path/filepath"
24		"strings"
25	
26		"github.com/spf13/afero"

29		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"

32		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins"
33		kustomizev2scaffolds "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/common/kustomize/v2/scaffolds"
34		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/deploy-image/v1alpha1/scaffolds/internal/templates/api"
35		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/deploy-image/v1alpha1/scaffolds/internal/templates/config/samples"
36		"sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/deploy-image/v1alpha1/scaffolds/internal/templates/controllers"
37		golangv4scaffolds "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds"
38	)
39	
40	var _ plugins.Scaffolder = &apiScaffolder{}
41	
42	// apiScaffolder contains configuration for generating scaffolding for Go type
43	// representing the API and controller that implements the behavior for the API.
44	type apiScaffolder struct {
45		config    config.Config
46		resource  resource.Resource
47		image     string
48		command   string
49		port      string
50		runAsUser string
51	
52		// fs is the filesystem that will be used by the scaffolder
53		fs machinery.Filesystem
54	}
55	
56	// NewDeployImageScaffolder returns a new Scaffolder for declarative
57	func NewDeployImageScaffolder(cfg config.Config, res resource.Resource, image,
58		command, port, runAsUser string,
59	) plugins.Scaffolder {
60		return &apiScaffolder{
61			config:    cfg,
62			resource:  res,
63			image:     image,
64			command:   command,
65			port:      port,
66			runAsUser: runAsUser,
67		}
68	}
69	
70	// InjectFS implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
71	func (s *apiScaffolder) InjectFS(fs machinery.Filesystem) {
72		s.fs = fs
73	}
74	
75	// Scaffold implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
76	func (s *apiScaffolder) Scaffold() error {
77		log.Info("Writing scaffold for you to edit...")
78	
79		if err := s.scaffoldCreateAPI(); err != nil {
80			return err
81		}
82	
83		// Define the boilerplate file path
84		boilerplatePath := filepath.Join("hack", "boilerplate.go.txt")
85	
86		// Load the boilerplate
87		boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, boilerplatePath)
88		if err != nil {
89			if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
90				log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
91					"This file is used to generate the license header in the project.\n"+
92					"Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
93					"add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
94					"file_path", boilerplatePath, "error", err)
95				boilerplate = []byte("")
96			} else {
97				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding API/controller: failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
98			}
99		}
100	
101		// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
102		scaffold := machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
103			machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
104			machinery.WithBoilerplate(string(boilerplate)),
105			machinery.WithResource(&s.resource),
106		)
107	
108		if err := scaffold.Execute(
109			&api.Types{Port: s.port},
110		); err != nil {
111			return fmt.Errorf("error updating APIs: %w", err)
112		}
113	
114		if err := scaffold.Execute(
115			&samples.CRDSample{Port: s.port},
116		); err != nil {
117			return fmt.Errorf("error updating config/samples: %w", err)
118		}
119	
120		controller := &controllers.Controller{
121			ControllerRuntimeVersion: golangv4scaffolds.ControllerRuntimeVersion,
122		}
123	
124		if err := scaffold.Execute(
125			controller,
126		); err != nil {
127			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding controller: %w", err)
128		}
129	
130		if err := s.updateControllerCode(*controller); err != nil {
131			return fmt.Errorf("error updating controller: %w", err)
132		}
133	
134		defaultMainPath := "cmd/main.go"
135		if err := s.updateMainByAddingEventRecorder(defaultMainPath); err != nil {
136			return fmt.Errorf("error updating main.go: %w", err)
137		}
138	
139		if err := scaffold.Execute(
140			&controllers.ControllerTest{Port: s.port},
141		); err != nil {
142			return fmt.Errorf("error creating controller/**_controller_test.go: %w", err)
143		}
144	
145		return s.addEnvVarIntoManager()
146	}
147	
148	// addEnvVarIntoManager will update the config/manager/manager.yaml by adding
149	// a new ENV VAR for to store the image informed which will be used in the
150	// controller to create the Pod for the Kind
151	func (s *apiScaffolder) addEnvVarIntoManager() error {
152		managerPath := filepath.Join("config", "manager", "manager.yaml")
153		err := util.ReplaceInFile(managerPath, `env:`, `env:`)
154		if err != nil {
155			if err = util.InsertCode(managerPath, `name: manager`, "\n        env:"); err != nil {
156				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding env key in config/manager/manager.yaml")
157			}
158		}
159	
160		if err = util.InsertCode(managerPath, `env:`,
161			fmt.Sprintf(envVarTemplate, strings.ToUpper(s.resource.Kind), s.image)); err != nil {
162			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding env key in config/manager/manager.yaml")
163		}
164	
165		return nil
166	}
167	
168	// scaffoldCreateAPI will reuse the code from the kustomize and base golang
169	// plugins to do the default scaffolds which an API is created
170	func (s *apiScaffolder) scaffoldCreateAPI() error {
171		if err := s.scaffoldCreateAPIFromGolang(); err != nil {
172			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding golang files for the new API: %w", err)
173		}
174	
175		if err := s.scaffoldCreateAPIFromKustomize(); err != nil {
176			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding kustomize manifests for the new API: %w", err)
177		}
178		return nil
179	}
180	
181	// TODO: replace this implementation by creating its own MainUpdater
182	// which will have its own controller template which set the recorder so that we can use it
183	// in the reconciliation to create an event inside for the finalizer
184	func (s *apiScaffolder) updateMainByAddingEventRecorder(defaultMainPath string) error {
185		if err := util.InsertCode(
186			defaultMainPath,
187			fmt.Sprintf(
188				`%sReconciler{
189			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
190			Scheme: mgr.GetScheme(),`, s.resource.Kind),
191			fmt.Sprintf(recorderTemplate, strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind)),
192		); err != nil {
193			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding event recorder in %q: %w", defaultMainPath, err)
194		}
195	
196		return nil
197	}
198	
199	// updateControllerCode will update the code generate on the template to add the Container information
200	func (s *apiScaffolder) updateControllerCode(controller controllers.Controller) error {
201		if err := util.ReplaceInFile(
202			controller.Path,
203			"//TODO: scaffold container",
204			fmt.Sprintf(containerTemplate, // value for the image
205				strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind), // value for the name of the container
206			),
207		); err != nil {
208			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding container in the controller path %q: %w",
209				controller.Path, err)
210		}
211	
212		// Scaffold the command if informed
213		if len(s.command) > 0 {
214			// TODO: improve it to be an spec in the sample and api instead so that
215			// users can change the values
216			var res string
217			for value := range strings.SplitSeq(s.command, ",") {
218				res += fmt.Sprintf(" \"%s\",", strings.TrimSpace(value))
219			}
220			// remove the latest ,
221			res = res[:len(res)-1]
222			// remove the first space to not fail in the go fmt ./...
223			res = strings.TrimLeft(res, " ")
224	
225			if err := util.InsertCode(controller.Path, `SecurityContext: &corev1.SecurityContext{
226								RunAsNonRoot:             ptr.To(true),
227								AllowPrivilegeEscalation: ptr.To(false),
228								Capabilities: &corev1.Capabilities{
229									Drop: []corev1.Capability{
230										"ALL",
231									},
232								},
233							},`, fmt.Sprintf(commandTemplate, res)); err != nil {
234				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding command in the  controller path %q: %w",
235					controller.Path, err)
236			}
237		}
238	
239		// Scaffold the port if informed
240		if len(s.port) > 0 {
241			if err := util.InsertCode(
242				controller.Path,
243				`SecurityContext: &corev1.SecurityContext{
244								RunAsNonRoot:             ptr.To(true),
245								AllowPrivilegeEscalation: ptr.To(false),
246								Capabilities: &corev1.Capabilities{
247									Drop: []corev1.Capability{
248										"ALL",
249									},
250								},
251							},`,
252				fmt.Sprintf(
253					portTemplate,
254					strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind),
255					strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind)),
256			); err != nil {
257				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding container port in the controller path %q: %w",
258					controller.Path,
259					err)
260			}
261		}
262	
263		if len(s.runAsUser) > 0 {
264			if err := util.InsertCode(
265				controller.Path,
266				`RunAsNonRoot:             ptr.To(true),`,
267				fmt.Sprintf(runAsUserTemplate, s.runAsUser),
268			); err != nil {
269				return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding user-id in the controller path %q: %w",
270					controller.Path, err)
271			}
272		}
273	
274		return nil
275	}
276	
277	func (s *apiScaffolder) scaffoldCreateAPIFromKustomize() error {
278		kustomizeScaffolder := kustomizev2scaffolds.NewAPIScaffolder(
279			s.config,
280			s.resource,
281			true,
282		)
283	
284		kustomizeScaffolder.InjectFS(s.fs)
285	
286		if err := kustomizeScaffolder.Scaffold(); err != nil {
287			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding kustomize files for the APIs: %w", err)
288		}
289	
290		return nil
291	}
292	
293	func (s *apiScaffolder) scaffoldCreateAPIFromGolang() error {
294		golangV4Scaffolder := golangv4scaffolds.NewAPIScaffolder(s.config,
295			s.resource, true)
296		golangV4Scaffolder.InjectFS(s.fs)
297		if err := golangV4Scaffolder.Scaffold(); err != nil {
298			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding golang files for the APIs: %v", err)
299		}
300	
301		return nil
302	}
303	
304	const containerTemplate = `Containers: []corev1.Container{{
305							Image:           image,
306							Name:            "%s",
307							ImagePullPolicy: corev1.PullIfNotPresent,
308							// Ensure restrictive context for the container
309							// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/#restricted
310							SecurityContext: &corev1.SecurityContext{
311								RunAsNonRoot:             ptr.To(true),
312								AllowPrivilegeEscalation: ptr.To(false),
313								Capabilities: &corev1.Capabilities{
314									Drop: []corev1.Capability{
315										"ALL",
316									},
317								},
318							},
319						}}`
320	
321	const runAsUserTemplate = `
322								RunAsUser:                ptr.To(int64(%s)),`
323	
324	const commandTemplate = `
325							Command: []string{%s},`
326	
327	const portTemplate = `
328							Ports: []corev1.ContainerPort{{
329								ContainerPort: %s.Spec.ContainerPort,
330								Name:          "%s",
331							}},`
332	
333	const recorderTemplate = `
334			Recorder: mgr.GetEventRecorder("%s-controller"),`
335	
336	const envVarTemplate = `
337	        - name: %s_IMAGE
338	          value: %s`

content.text:
Now I need to add YEAR substitution logic. The scaffolders (init.go, api.go, webhook.go, deploy-image/api.go) read the boilerplate from disk and pass it to the scaffolding machinery. I need them to replace `YEAR` with the current year after reading the file. Let me create a helper function for this in the machinery package, as it's used in multiple places.

Let me check the machinery interfaces first:

content.text:
Now, looking at the pattern, the best place to add the YEAR substitution would be in the `scaffold.go` `buildFileModel` method, specifically when we build the model from a template. But that's only for template-built files, not for the boilerplate file itself.

Actually, looking at the flow more carefully:

1. `init.go` writes `hack/boilerplate.go.txt` with the template using YEAR
2. Then reads the boilerplate file back and uses it as the boilerplate text for scaffolding other files

The key insight is that when the boilerplate is read from disk (in init.go, api.go, webhook.go, deploy-image), we need to replace `YEAR` with the current year. Let me add a helper function to the machinery package for this and use it in all the scaffolders.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The cleanest approach: add the YEAR replacement to the machinery's `injector.injectInto` method. That way, whenever a boilerplate is injected into a template, the YEAR is replaced automatically.

Looking at the injector, the boilerplate string is stored as `s.injector.boilerplate`. The replacement should happen when `WithBoilerplate` is called or when `injectInto` is called.

Actually, I think the best approach is to add a helper function `ReplaceYear` in the machinery package and use it in all the scaffolders where boilerplate is loaded. Let me add this to scaffold.go:
I'll modify `WithBoilerplate` to perform the YEAR substitution. That way, all scaffolders that use `WithBoilerplate` will automatically get the YEAR replaced:

/*

limitations under the License.
